step: Digital-twin workforce (synthetic individuals matched to member workforce)

sources.dataset: Populus synth-pop DB, US_2025.v0 (box, via Tailscale)
sources.note: 12,000 synthetic workers from 699 member tracts; workplace-linked agents only; household coordinates from place.geom (PostGIS).
Tract sampling probabilities (∝ modeled members) + LODES workforce age/earnings targets.

method: PPS sample of member tracts (∝ modeled members, seeded); within each, take an UNBIASED md5-ordered sample, keep working-age adults (16–74), and dedupe to one per household → a raw worker pool. The pool is JOINTLY reweighted to THIS employer's LODES workforce AGE shape (SA01–03, working-age window 16–74) and a member-representative HOUSEHOLD INCOME target (the member workforce's LODES earnings shares, lifted individual→household by a 1.6× earner uplift) by sampling ∝ (age × income) target/observed. Sex, race and ethnicity are NOT reweighted — they carry the real signal of who the members are. Each twin is a synthetic person at its household point.
integrity: Synthetic individuals (digital twins) — modeled, not real people; a stratified sample matched to the member workforce.

Workforce proxy: working-age adults (16–74), one per household — excludes children and the elderly and avoids overlapping same-household dots. (A workplace-link filter is more precise but ~18s/tract; the age window already removes non-workers by age.)
Within-tract draw is md5-ordered (unbiased), not agent_id-ordered.

Age reweighted to the LODES workforce shape (16–74); household income reweighted toward member households (a residential draw over-states income — it's the AREA median, but member households hold a lower-wage retail worker). The income uplift (1.6×, range 1.4–1.8) is a documented assumption reconciled by a census in Mode 1B.
Sex / race / ethnicity left at their values and validated, not adjusted.
Absurd ages (>100) and absurd incomes (≥$500K) dropped/nulled per the db-access rules.
Coordinates are household points (place.geom); placement is the synthpop's own.
Member-weighting is the MODELED commute geography, not factual member addresses.
race/sex/hispanic are integer codes; M/F (sex 0/1) mapping unconfirmed.
